paper_title: Microalgae Culturing and Chemical Extraction Methods

methods.description: Culturing and maintaining microalgae species for antimicrobial activity screening.

steps.description: Microalgae (46 species) were selected from the Stazione Zoologica Anton Dohrn culture collection for culturing and antimicrobial activity screening.
steps.searchable_summary: select microalgae species from culture collection
inputs: Stazione Zoologica Anton Dohrn culture collection

steps.description: Diatoms and flagellates were grown in Guillard's f/2 medium and dinoflagellates in Keller medium in 10 L polycarbonate bottles.
steps.searchable_summary: grow diatoms and flagellates in Guillard's f/2 medium, dinoflagellates in Keller medium

steps.description: Species were grown in control, nitrogen-and/or phosphate-starved media.
steps.searchable_summary: grow species in control, nitrogen-starved, and phosphate-starved media

steps.description: Cultures were kept in a climate chamber at 19 °C at 100 µmol photons m−2 s−1 at a 12:12 h light:dark cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain cultures in climate chamber with specific conditions

steps.description: Cultures were centrifuged for 30 min at 4 °C at 3000 g and pellets were kept at −80 °C until chemical extraction.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge cultures and store pellets at −80 °C

methods.description: Chemical extraction using Amberlite XAD16N resin to extract hydrophobic compounds from microalgal samples.

steps.description: 50 mL of distilled water was added to the microalgal pellets and samples were sonicated at 30 kHz for 30 s twice.
steps.searchable_summary: add water and sonicate microalgal pellets

steps.description: The same volume of acetone was added and samples were evaporated under nitrogen stream down to half of their volume.
steps.searchable_summary: add acetone and evaporate samples under nitrogen

steps.description: 1 g of Amberlite XAD16N resin was added to each sample and mixed for 50 min at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: add Amberlite XAD16N resin and mix samples

steps.description: Samples were centrifuged (15 min at 3500 g at room temperature) and 18 mL of water were added to the resin for a washing step.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge samples and wash resin with water

steps.description: The resin was incubated with 10 mL acetone for 50 min and centrifuged to settle the resin.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate resin with acetone and centrifuge

steps.description: The supernatants were freeze-dried and stored at −20 °C until screening.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze-dry supernatants and store

methods.description: Chemical extraction using a combination of water, acetone, and dichloromethane to separate organic and water phases from microalgal samples.

steps.description: 2 mL of distilled water per each g of fresh sample weight were added to each microalgal pellets and sonicated at 30 kHz for 30 s twice.
steps.searchable_summary: add water and sonicate microalgal pellets

steps.description: A volume of acetone was added and samples were centrifuged at 3600 rpm for 6 min at 4 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: add acetone and centrifuge samples

steps.description: The supernatant was transferred in clean tubes and stored on ice.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer supernatant to clean tubes and store on ice

steps.description: Water and acetone were added again to the remaining pellets and the centrifugation step was repeated for two other times.
steps.searchable_summary: repeat water and acetone addition and centrifugation

steps.description: A volume of dichloromethane was added to the recovered supernatant, mixed and centrifuged at 3600 rpm for 6′ at 15 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: add dichloromethane, mix, and centrifuge supernatant

steps.description: The organic phase was stored on ice, while the extraction with dichloromethane was repeated two times.
steps.searchable_summary: store organic phase on ice and repeat extraction

steps.description: The recovered water was freeze-dried and stored as water samples.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze-dry and store water samples

steps.description: The organic samples were pooled and treated with anhydrous sodium sulfate to remove water residues and dried under vacuum.
steps.searchable_summary: treat organic samples with sodium sulfate and dry under vacuum

methods.description: Evaluation of cytotoxicity of microalgal extracts on human hepatocellular liver carcinoma cells.

steps.description: 20,000 HepG2 cells were seeded per well, grown overnight, and then incubated with 50 μg/mL test extract.
steps.searchable_summary: seed HepG2 cells and incubate with test extract

steps.description: Ten μL of CellTiter 96® AQueous One Solution Reagent was added and plates were incubated for 1 h.
steps.searchable_summary: add CellTiter 96 reagent and incubate

steps.description: Absorbance was measured at 485 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: measure absorbance at 485 nm

methods.description: Testing the antibacterial activity of microalgal extracts against MRSA and E. coli.

steps.description: Thawed stock inocula suspensions from cryovials of each microorganism were streaked onto Luria-Bertani agar plates and incubated at 37 °C overnight.
steps.searchable_summary: streak microorganisms on agar plates and incubate

steps.description: Single colonies were inoculated into Luria-Bertani broth medium and incubated overnight at 37 °C with shaking.
steps.searchable_summary: inoculate colonies in broth medium and incubate with shaking

steps.description: 90 μL/well of the diluted inoculum were mixed with microalgal organic extract and LB medium.
steps.searchable_summary: mix inoculum with microalgal extract and LB medium

steps.description: Absorbance was measured at 612 nm at T0 and after 20 h incubation at 37 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: measure absorbance at 612 nm before and after incubation
